description: The structured `result` action output. Discriminated by `phase` into three variants: Phase 1 (`branch-management`) on a push to the target branch, Phase 2 (`validation`) on a push to the release branch, and Phase 3 (`publishing`) on the merge of the release PR. Every variant carries the same shared top-level fields (`$schema`, `schemaVersion`, `phase`, `status`, `noop`, `succeeded`, `hasFailures`, `dryRun`) plus a phase-specific payload.
title: Silk Release Action output

$schema.description: URL of the hosted JSON Schema this output conforms to. Editors and json-schema-aware consumers use this for hover docs and validation.
$schema.title: JSON Schema URL

schemaVersion.description: In-band schema version. Bumped only on a breaking JSON-shape change (removed/renamed field, changed type) — additive fields do not bump it.
schemaVersion.title: Schema version

phase.description: `branch-management` identifies this as a Phase 1 output.
phase.title: Phase discriminator

noop.description: True when the phase had nothing to do (no changesets, no release branch updates, no targets to publish).
noop.title: No-op

succeeded.description: True when the phase completed cleanly with no failures.
succeeded.title: Succeeded

hasFailures.description: True when at least one operation in the phase failed. Set on any failure, including partial ones.
hasFailures.title: Has failures

dryRun.description: True when the action ran with `dry-run: true`. Phase 3 publishes nothing in dry-run mode; Phases 1 and 2 still observe and report, but mutations (branch updates, PR creation/updating) are suppressed.
dryRun.title: Dry run

BranchManagementOutput.description: The structured `result` output emitted when the action runs in the branch-management phase (Phase 1). Triggered by a push to the target branch; ensures/creates the release branch and the release PR.
BranchManagementOutput.title: Branch Management output (Phase 1)

ReleaseStatus.description: Coarse human-readable status label, derived from the machine flags. `no-op`: nothing to do this run; `success`: the phase completed cleanly; `partial`: the phase completed but with at least one failure; `failed`: the phase did not succeed and did not produce partial results. Consumers needing failure granularity should read `succeeded`/`hasFailures` and the phase payload instead.
ReleaseStatus.title: Release status

BranchManagementPayload.description: Phase 1 outcome — the release-branch ensure/create result, the release PR (created or updated, null when no changesets), and the changesets observed in `.changeset/` with their derived per-package bumps.
BranchManagementPayload.title: Branch Management payload

name.description: The git branch name the release PR targets and changeset versioning lands on.
name.title: Release branch name

existed.description: True when the release branch already existed before this run; false when it was just created.
existed.title: Branch existed

created.description: True when this run created the release branch.
created.title: Branch created

updated.description: True when this run pushed new commits to the release branch (changeset version bumps, etc.).
updated.title: Branch updated

hasConflicts.description: True when the release branch could not be cleanly fast-forwarded over the target branch and a merge conflict was detected.
hasConflicts.title: Has conflicts

BranchManagementReleaseBranch.description: The state of the release branch after Phase 1 — whether it existed, was created/updated, and any conflicts.
BranchManagementReleaseBranch.title: Release branch state

number.description: The GitHub PR number for the release PR.
number.title: Release PR number

url.description: The HTML URL of the release PR on GitHub.
url.title: Release PR URL

action.description: Whether this run created the release PR or updated an existing one.
action.title: Release PR action

BranchManagementReleasePr.description: The release PR's number, URL, and the action this run took on it. Null when no PR was created (no changesets to release).
BranchManagementReleasePr.title: Release PR state

count.description: The number of changeset files observed in the `.changeset/` directory.
count.title: Changeset count

packages.description: The set of packages the observed changesets bump, with the derived semver bump per package.
packages.title: Changeset packages

BranchManagementChangesets.description: Summary of the changesets observed this run — total count and the per-package bump types.
BranchManagementChangesets.title: Changesets observed

name.description: The npm package name the changeset bumps.
name.title: Package name

BranchManagementChangesetPackage.description: One package affected by the observed changesets, with its derived semver bump.
BranchManagementChangesetPackage.title: Changeset package

ChangesetsBumpType.description: The semver bump derived from the changeset(s) for this package: `major`, `minor`, or `patch`.
ChangesetsBumpType.title: Changeset bump type

$schema.description: URL of the hosted JSON Schema this output conforms to. Editors and json-schema-aware consumers use this for hover docs and validation.
$schema.title: JSON Schema URL

schemaVersion.description: In-band schema version. Bumped only on a breaking JSON-shape change (removed/renamed field, changed type) — additive fields do not bump it.
schemaVersion.title: Schema version

phase.description: `validation` identifies this as a Phase 2 output.
phase.title: Phase discriminator

noop.description: True when the phase had nothing to do (no changesets, no release branch updates, no targets to publish).
noop.title: No-op

succeeded.description: True when the phase completed cleanly with no failures.
succeeded.title: Succeeded

hasFailures.description: True when at least one operation in the phase failed. Set on any failure, including partial ones.
hasFailures.title: Has failures

dryRun.description: True when the action ran with `dry-run: true`. Phase 3 publishes nothing in dry-run mode; Phases 1 and 2 still observe and report, but mutations (branch updates, PR creation/updating) are suppressed.
dryRun.title: Dry run

ValidationOutput.description: The structured `result` output emitted when the action runs in the validation phase (Phase 2). Triggered by a push to the release branch; runs build validation, publish dry-runs, release-notes extraction, and emits the unified Release Validation Summary check-run.
ValidationOutput.title: Validation output (Phase 2)

checks.description: The five-row Validation Checks table — one entry per validation step run this phase.
checks.title: Validation checks

findings.description: Every non-pass outcome surfaced by the validation checks, projected for the release PR comment.
findings.title: Findings

ValidationPayload.description: Phase 2 outcome — the build-validation result, the Validation Checks table, every non-pass finding, the build-centric publish preview (per-package, per-build, per-target readiness), and the unified Release Validation Summary check-run URL.
ValidationPayload.title: Validation payload

passed.description: True when every released package built successfully.
passed.title: Build validation passed

packageCount.description: Number of packages built and validated.
packageCount.title: Package count

ValidationBuildValidation.description: Summary of the build-validation step: whether every package built, and how many were built.
ValidationBuildValidation.title: Build validation summary

name.description: Row label for the Validation Checks table.
name.title: Check name

status.description: `pass` — the check passed; `warning` — the check raised a non-blocking warning; `error` — the check failed and blocks the release.
status.title: Check status

outcome.description: Short human-readable summary of the check's outcome (e.g. counts, registry names, error class).
outcome.title: Outcome message

anyOf.description: URL pointing to detail for this check (a check-run URL or external report). Null when no URL applies.
anyOf.title: Check URL

ValidationCheck.description: One row of the five-row Validation Checks table shown in the release PR comment and the unified check-run summary.
ValidationCheck.title: Validation check row

severity.description: `error` — fails the check and blocks the release; `warning` — advisory only, does not block.
severity.title: Finding severity

check.description: The validation check that produced this finding (e.g. `Build Validation`, `Publish Validation`).
check.title: Owning check

message.description: Human-readable description of the finding, suitable for direct display in the release PR comment.
message.title: Finding message

ValidationFinding.description: Every non-pass validation outcome — `error` fails the check and blocks the release; `warning` is advisory. Findings are projected for the release PR comment and for downstream consumers of the `result` output.
ValidationFinding.title: Validation finding

anyOf.description: The npm package name the finding concerns. Null for global (non-package-specific) findings.
anyOf.title: Package name

anyOf.description: The build output directory the finding concerns, package-relative. Null for findings not tied to a specific build directory.
anyOf.title: Build directory

ValidationFindingScope.description: What a non-pass finding concerns: which package and which of its build directories. `package` is null for global findings; `directory` is null for findings not tied to a specific build directory.
ValidationFindingScope.title: Validation finding scope

npmReady.description: True when every npm publish target passed its dry-run probe.
npmReady.title: npm ready

githubPackagesReady.description: True when every GitHub Packages publish target passed its dry-run probe.
githubPackagesReady.title: GitHub Packages ready

totalTargets.description: Total number of publish targets across every released package and every registry.
totalTargets.title: Total targets

readyTargets.description: Number of publish targets that passed their dry-run probe.
readyTargets.title: Ready targets

packages.description: The packages being released this run, with their builds and per-target readiness.
packages.title: Released packages

ValidationPublish.description: Build-centric publish preview — per-registry readiness rollup plus the full per-package, per-build, per-target breakdown.
ValidationPublish.title: Publish preview

name.description: The npm package name being released.
name.title: Package name

version.description: The new semver version this release would publish.
version.title: New version

anyOf.description: The previously published version this release is bumped from. Null when this is the first publish.
anyOf.title: Base version

anyOf.description: Number of changesets contributing to this package's bump. Null when unknown.
anyOf.title: Changeset count

ready.description: True when every publish target for this package's builds passed its dry-run probe.
ready.title: Ready

versionOnly.description: True when the package has no publish targets — only a GitHub release (and tag) is produced. Used for repos that version a package but don't publish it to a registry.
versionOnly.title: Version-only

builds.description: The unique output directories this package produces, one entry per build.
builds.title: Builds

ValidationPublishPackage.description: A package being released this run, with its bump type, builds, per-target readiness, and the extracted release notes.
ValidationPublishPackage.title: Released package

ValidationBumpType.description: `major`/`minor`/`patch` — standard semver bump derived from the changesets; `new` — the package has never been published before; `unknown` — the bump could not be derived (e.g. changesets unreadable).
ValidationBumpType.title: Bump type

directory.description: Package-relative path to the build's output directory. One build is produced per unique output directory; the tarball is packed once and shared across all targets publishing this directory.
directory.title: Build directory

anyOf.description: Size of the packed tarball in bytes. Null when the dry-run did not report it.
anyOf.title: Packed size (bytes)

anyOf.description: Size of the unpacked contents in bytes. Null when the dry-run did not report it.
anyOf.title: Unpacked size (bytes)

anyOf.description: Number of files in the packed tarball. Null when the dry-run did not report it.
anyOf.title: File count

targets.description: The registry targets this build would publish to, with per-target readiness.
targets.title: Publish targets

ValidationBuild.description: One unique output directory of a released package. The tarball is packed once and shared across all registry targets publishing this directory; per-target readiness is enumerated in `targets`.
ValidationBuild.title: Build

componentCount.description: Number of components (direct + transitive dependencies) in the BOM. 0 is legitimate for a dependency-free package.
componentCount.title: Component count

ntiaCompliant.description: True when the BOM satisfies every NTIA minimum-elements field. False when at least one field is missing — see `missingNtiaFields`.
ntiaCompliant.title: NTIA compliant

missingNtiaFields.description: Names of the NTIA minimum-elements fields the BOM is missing. Empty when `ntiaCompliant` is true.
missingNtiaFields.title: Missing NTIA fields

ValidationBuildSbom.description: Per-build SBOM preview: component count, NTIA minimum-elements compliance, and the list of missing NTIA fields when not compliant.
ValidationBuildSbom.title: SBOM preview

registry.description: The registry endpoint this target would publish to.
registry.title: Registry URL

status.description: `ready` — the dry-run publish probe succeeded and the target is ready to publish; `skipped` — the target was intentionally not probed (e.g. unconfigured or filtered out); `failed` — the dry-run probe failed and the target would not publish.
status.title: Target readiness

access.description: `public` — the package would publish publicly; `restricted` — the package would publish privately (scoped, restricted access).
access.title: Access level

provenance.description: True when the target supports and would emit npm OIDC sigstore provenance attestations.
provenance.title: Provenance

ValidationBuildTarget.description: Per-registry publish readiness for a single build directory: `ready` / `skipped` / `failed`, plus access level and provenance support.
ValidationBuildTarget.title: Publish target

ValidationReleaseNotes.description: Discriminated outcome of reading the package's CHANGELOG.md (already populated by `changeset version`) and locating the section for the new version: `found`, `no-changelog`, `version-not-found`, or `error`.
ValidationReleaseNotes.title: Release notes extraction

status.description: The CHANGELOG.md was found and the section for the new version was successfully extracted.
status.title: Found

content.description: The extracted Markdown content of the CHANGELOG.md section for the new version.
content.title: Release notes content

ReleaseNotesFound.description: The CHANGELOG.md exists and the section for the new version was located and extracted.
ReleaseNotesFound.title: Release notes found

status.description: The package has no CHANGELOG.md file — no release notes can be extracted.
status.title: No CHANGELOG.md

ReleaseNotesNoChangelog.description: The package has no CHANGELOG.md file. This is non-fatal; the GitHub release is created with the version-bump summary instead.
ReleaseNotesNoChangelog.title: Release notes — no CHANGELOG.md

status.description: The CHANGELOG.md exists but no section for the new version was found.
status.title: Version not found

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the version section was not located (e.g. parser couldn't find a matching heading).
reason.title: Reason

ReleaseNotesVersionNotFound.description: The CHANGELOG.md exists but no section for the new version was found.
ReleaseNotesVersionNotFound.title: Release notes — version not found

status.description: An error occurred while attempting to read or parse the CHANGELOG.md.
status.title: Error

message.description: The error message from the failed read/parse operation.
message.title: Error message

ReleaseNotesError.description: An error occurred while attempting to read or parse the CHANGELOG.md.
ReleaseNotesError.title: Release notes — error

url.description: The HTML URL of the unified Release Validation Summary check-run.
url.title: Check-run URL

ValidationCheckRun.description: The unified Release Validation Summary check-run produced by the validation phase. Null when no check-run was created.
ValidationCheckRun.title: Validation check-run

$schema.description: URL of the hosted JSON Schema this output conforms to. Editors and json-schema-aware consumers use this for hover docs and validation.
$schema.title: JSON Schema URL

schemaVersion.description: In-band schema version. Bumped only on a breaking JSON-shape change (removed/renamed field, changed type) — additive fields do not bump it.
schemaVersion.title: Schema version

phase.description: `publishing` identifies this as a Phase 3 output.
phase.title: Phase discriminator

noop.description: True when the phase had nothing to do (no changesets, no release branch updates, no targets to publish).
noop.title: No-op

succeeded.description: True when the phase completed cleanly with no failures.
succeeded.title: Succeeded

hasFailures.description: True when at least one operation in the phase failed. Set on any failure, including partial ones.
hasFailures.title: Has failures

dryRun.description: True when the action ran with `dry-run: true`. Phase 3 publishes nothing in dry-run mode; Phases 1 and 2 still observe and report, but mutations (branch updates, PR creation/updating) are suppressed.
dryRun.title: Dry run

PublishingOutput.description: The structured `result` output emitted when the action runs in the publishing phase (Phase 3). Triggered by the merge of the release PR; publishes to every configured registry, generates SBOM/provenance attestations, and creates GitHub releases and tags.
PublishingOutput.title: Publishing output (Phase 3)

packages.description: The packages processed by the publishing phase, with their per-target outcomes.
packages.title: Published packages

tags.description: Git tags created by the publishing phase (one per released package or one for the whole release, depending on workflow).
tags.title: Release tags

releases.description: GitHub releases created by the publishing phase.
releases.title: GitHub releases

PublishingPayload.description: Phase 3 outcome — the per-package publish results (with attestation URLs and tarball digests), the git tags created, and the GitHub releases created.
PublishingPayload.title: Publishing payload

name.description: The npm package name that was (or would have been) published.
name.title: Package name

version.description: The semver version this run published.
version.title: Published version

status.description: `published` — at least one target accepted the publish; `skipped` — every target was skipped (no work landed); `failed` — every target either failed or skipped, but at least one failed.
status.title: Package publish status

targets.description: The per-registry publish outcomes for this package.
targets.title: Publish targets

anyOf.description: Integrity hash of the tarball as published (the `dist.integrity` field on the npm registry, typically `sha512-...`). Null when the publish did not produce a comparable digest (failed/skipped).
anyOf.title: Tarball digest

PublishPackage.description: A package that was processed by the publishing phase, with its per-target outcomes, attestation URLs, and the integrity digest of the published tarball.
PublishPackage.title: Published package

PublishPackageSkipReason.description: `already-published-identical` — the version is already published and the tarball digest matches what would be published; `already-published-unknown` — the version is already published but the on-registry tarball digest could not be compared. Null when the package was not skipped.
PublishPackageSkipReason.title: Skip reason

registry.description: The registry endpoint this target published to.
registry.title: Registry URL

status.description: `published` — the package was successfully published to this target; `skipped` — the publish was intentionally not attempted (e.g. dry-run, no token, already-published); `failed` — the publish was attempted and failed.
status.title: Publish status

anyOf.description: URL of the published artifact on the registry. Null when the publish did not produce a discoverable URL.
anyOf.title: Published artifact URL

anyOf.description: Error message when `status` is `failed`. Null on `published` or `skipped`.
anyOf.title: Error message

PublishTarget.description: Per-registry publish outcome — what was attempted, whether it succeeded, and the URL of the published artifact.
PublishTarget.title: Published target

anyOf.description: URL of the npm OIDC sigstore provenance attestation. Null when no provenance was emitted.
anyOf.title: Provenance attestation URL

anyOf.description: URL of the CycloneDX SBOM attestation uploaded to the artifact store. Null when no SBOM attestation was emitted.
anyOf.title: SBOM attestation URL

anyOf.description: URL of the GitHub artifact-attestation (`gh attestation verify`). Null when no GitHub attestation was emitted.
anyOf.title: GitHub attestation URL

PublishPackageAttestations.description: Per-package attestation URLs emitted during publishing — provenance, SBOM, and the GitHub attestation.
PublishPackageAttestations.title: Attestations

name.description: The git tag name created for the release.
name.title: Tag name

sha.description: The commit SHA the tag points at.
sha.title: Tag SHA

PublishingTag.description: A git tag created for the release.
PublishingTag.title: Release tag

tag.description: The git tag the GitHub release is attached to.
tag.title: Tag

url.description: The HTML URL of the GitHub release.
url.title: Release URL

PublishingRelease.description: A GitHub release created by the publishing phase.
PublishingRelease.title: GitHub release
